analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet claims the EU and UN are natural partners and leading defenders of multilateralism and a rules-based international order, tagged to UNGA and framed as being stronger together. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – not directly; no historical success or overcoming obstacles referenced. EU2 (EU is united) – implicit unity is hinted by "StrongerTogether," but the focus is external partnership with the UN, not intra-EU unity; insufficient evidence. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – no citizen-focused, internal policy benefits mentioned. EU4 (avant-garde actor) – not about pioneering regulation or future challenges. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – it does not highlight human rights/democracy specifically. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – yes: it positions the EU as a global proponent/defender of multilateralism and a rules-based order and as a natural partner to the UN, which fits contributing to global cooperation and better governance. Conclusion: Only EU6 applies.
